title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Clinical programmers working R often face common challenge migrating SAS: SAS, single ... block can assign multiple variables one condition. R, traditional approaches like case_when() fifelse() force repeat condition every variable — increasing QC risk reducing readability. sasif solves bringing SAS-style / ELSE / ELSE control flow R’s data.table ecosystem. One condition governs assignments block — just like SAS. vignette walks three real-world ADaM derivation scenarios: ADSL — Population flags treatment variables ADLB — Laboratory value categorisation ADAE — Treatment-emergent adverse event flags

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
code: library(sasif) library(data.table) #> Warning: package 'data.table' was built under R version 4.5.2

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: typical ADSL derivation, subject treatment arm, multiple variables need assigned simultaneously — population flags, treatment labels, numeric codes, treatment dates. traditional R, every variable requires repeated condition: condition ever changes, must update 8 places. Miss one derivation silently diverges — real risk regulated environments.
code: # ❌ Traditional R — condition repeated for every variable adsl <- adsl %>% mutate(   SAFFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   SAFFLN  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ 1),   TRT01A  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ ACTARMCD),   TRT01AN = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ 1),   ITTFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   FASFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   RANDFL  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   PPFL    = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y")   # Same condition written 8 times — high QC risk )

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: 10 variables derived single condition block. Clean, readable, audit-friendly — exactly like SAS ... .
code: # Create sample ADSL data adsl <- data.table(   USUBJID  = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04"),   ACTARMCD = c("TRTA", "TRTA", "SCRNFAIL", "TRTA"),   RFSTDTC  = c("2024-01-10", "2024-01-15", NA, "2024-01-20"),   RFENDTC  = c("2024-06-10", "2024-06-15", NA, "2024-06-20") )  # ✅ sasif — condition written ONCE, governs all assignments ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTA",     SAFFL   = "Y",     SAFFLN  = 1,     TRT01A  = "Treatment A",     TRT01AN = 1,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     TRTEDT  = as.Date(RFENDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     ITTFL   = "Y",     FASFL   = "Y",     RANDFL  = "Y",     PPFL    = "Y"   ) )  print(ADSL[, .(USUBJID, ACTARMCD, SAFFL, TRT01A, TRT01AN, ITTFL, FASFL)]) #>    USUBJID ACTARMCD  SAFFL      TRT01A TRT01AN  ITTFL  FASFL #>     <char>   <char> <char>      <char>   <num> <char> <char> #> 1:     S01     TRTA      Y Treatment A       1      Y      Y #> 2:     S02     TRTA      Y Treatment A       1      Y      Y #> 3:     S03 SCRNFAIL   <NA>        <NA>      NA   <NA>   <NA> #> 4:     S04     TRTA      Y Treatment A       1      Y      Y

what: Scenario 2 — ADSL: Multi-Arm Treatment Assignment (IF / ELSE IF / ELSE)
title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: study multiple treatment arms, use full / ELSE / ELSE chain. first matching condition wins — others skipped: Notice TRT01A (character label) TRT01AN (numeric code) derived together condition — repetition needed.
code: adsl2 <- data.table(   USUBJID  = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04", "S05"),   ACTARMCD = c("TRTA", "TRTB", "TRTC", "TRTA", "TRTB"),   AGE      = c(35, 52, 67, 44, 58) )  ADSL2 <- data_step(adsl2,   if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTA",     TRT01A  = "Treatment A",     TRT01AN = 1   ),   else_if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTB",     TRT01A  = "Treatment B",     TRT01AN = 2   ),   else_do(     TRT01A  = "Placebo",     TRT01AN = 99   ) )  print(ADSL2[, .(USUBJID, ACTARMCD, TRT01A, TRT01AN)]) #>    USUBJID ACTARMCD      TRT01A TRT01AN #>     <char>   <char>      <char>   <num> #> 1:     S01     TRTA Treatment A       1 #> 2:     S02     TRTB Treatment B       2 #> 3:     S03     TRTC     Placebo      99 #> 4:     S04     TRTA Treatment A       1 #> 5:     S05     TRTB Treatment B       2

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Derive age category label numeric code one chain:
code: adsl3 <- data.table(   USUBJID = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04", "S05"),   AGE     = c(32, 45, 58, 71, 80) )  ADSL3 <- data_step(adsl3,   if_do(AGE <= 45,     AGECAT  = "YOUNG",     AGECATN = 1   ),   else_if_do(AGE <= 70,     AGECAT  = "MIDDLE",     AGECATN = 2   ),   else_do(     AGECAT  = "OLD",     AGECATN = 3   ) )  print(ADSL3[, .(USUBJID, AGE, AGECAT, AGECATN)]) #>    USUBJID   AGE AGECAT AGECATN #>     <char> <num> <char>   <num> #> 1:     S01    32  YOUNG       1 #> 2:     S02    45  YOUNG       1 #> 3:     S03    58 MIDDLE       2 #> 4:     S04    71    OLD       3 #> 5:     S05    80    OLD       3

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: common ADaM derivation — categorise lab values LOW, NORMAL, HIGH based reference ranges, derive character numeric category together: ALBCAT ALBCATN always consistent — derived condition, can never diverge.
code: adlb <- data.table(   USUBJID  = c("S01", "S01", "S02", "S02", "S03"),   LBTESTCD = c("ALB", "ALB", "ALB", "ALB", "ALB"),   AVAL     = c(2.8, 4.2, 5.6, 3.5, 1.9),   ANRLO    = c(3.5, 3.5, 3.5, 3.5, 3.5),   ANRHI    = c(5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 5.0) )  ADLB <- data_step(adlb,   if_do(LBTESTCD == "ALB" & AVAL < ANRLO,     ALBCAT  = "LOW",     ALBCATN = 1   ),   else_if_do(LBTESTCD == "ALB" & AVAL > ANRHI,     ALBCAT  = "HIGH",     ALBCATN = 2   ),   else_do(     ALBCAT  = "NORMAL",     ALBCATN = 3   ) )  print(ADLB[, .(USUBJID, LBTESTCD, AVAL, ANRLO, ANRHI, ALBCAT, ALBCATN)]) #>    USUBJID LBTESTCD  AVAL ANRLO ANRHI ALBCAT ALBCATN #>     <char>   <char> <num> <num> <num> <char>   <num> #> 1:     S01      ALB   2.8   3.5     5    LOW       1 #> 2:     S01      ALB   4.2   3.5     5 NORMAL       3 #> 3:     S02      ALB   5.6   3.5     5   HIGH       2 #> 4:     S02      ALB   3.5   3.5     5 NORMAL       3 #> 5:     S03      ALB   1.9   3.5     5    LOW       1

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Flag adverse events started treatment start date:
code: adae <- data.table(   USUBJID = c("S01", "S01", "S02", "S02", "S03"),   AEDECOD = c("Headache", "Nausea", "Fatigue", "Dizziness", "Rash"),   ASTDT   = as.Date(c("2024-01-15", "2023-12-01",                        "2024-01-20", "2024-02-10", "2024-01-25")),   TRTSDT  = as.Date(c("2024-01-10", "2024-01-10",                        "2024-01-15", "2024-01-15", "2024-01-20")),   TRTEDT  = as.Date(c("2024-06-10", "2024-06-10",                        "2024-06-15", "2024-06-15", "2024-06-20")) )  ADAE <- data_step(adae,   if_do(ASTDT >= TRTSDT & ASTDT <= TRTEDT,     TRTEMFL = "Y",     TRTEMA  = AEDECOD   ) )  print(ADAE[, .(USUBJID, AEDECOD, ASTDT, TRTSDT, TRTEMFL)]) #>    USUBJID   AEDECOD      ASTDT     TRTSDT TRTEMFL #>     <char>    <char>     <Date>     <Date>  <char> #> 1:     S01  Headache 2024-01-15 2024-01-10       Y #> 2:     S01    Nausea 2023-12-01 2024-01-10    <NA> #> 3:     S02   Fatigue 2024-01-20 2024-01-15       Y #> 4:     S02 Dizziness 2024-02-10 2024-01-15       Y #> 5:     S03      Rash 2024-01-25 2024-01-20       Y

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Use delete_if() remove rows explicitly — mirrors SAS DELETE statement makes intent clear code: records valid test codes scheduled visits retained.
code: adlb2 <- data.table(   USUBJID  = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04", "S05"),   LBTESTCD = c("ALB", NA,    "ALB", "ALB", NA),   VISIT    = c("WEEK 1", "WEEK 1", "UNSCHEDULED", "WEEK 2", "WEEK 4"),   AVAL     = c(4.2, 3.8, 5.1, 4.0, 3.5) )  ADLB2 <- data_step(adlb2,   delete_if(is.na(LBTESTCD)),   delete_if(VISIT == "UNSCHEDULED") )  print(ADLB2) #>    USUBJID LBTESTCD  VISIT  AVAL #>     <char>   <char> <char> <num> #> 1:     S01      ALB WEEK 1   4.2 #> 2:     S04      ALB WEEK 2   4.0

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Use if_independent() conditions mutually exclusive — condition evaluated , multiple flags can apply row simultaneously: Subject S04 (age 72, weight 43, diabetic) receives three flags — three conditions TRUE row simultaneously.
code: adsl4 <- data.table(   USUBJID = c("S01", "S02", "S03", "S04"),   AGE     = c(30, 68, 45, 72),   WEIGHTKG = c(48, 72, 55, 43),   DIABFL  = c("N", "Y", "N", "Y") )  ADSL4 <- data_step(adsl4,   if_independent(AGE > 65,       SENIORFL  = "Y"),   if_independent(WEIGHTKG < 50,  LOWWTFL   = "Y"),   if_independent(DIABFL == "Y",  COMORBFL  = "Y") )  print(ADSL4) #>    USUBJID   AGE WEIGHTKG DIABFL SENIORFL LOWWTFL COMORBFL #>     <char> <num>    <num> <char>   <char>  <char>   <char> #> 1:     S01    30       48      N     <NA>       Y     <NA> #> 2:     S02    68       72      Y        Y    <NA>        Y #> 3:     S03    45       55      N     <NA>    <NA>     <NA> #> 4:     S04    72       43      Y        Y       Y        Y

what: Key Principle: When to Use Which Function
title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: Important: mix if_do() chains if_independent() variable. if_independent() runs chain overwrite earlier assignments. Use one approach consistently per variable.

title: Clinical ADaM Derivations with sasif
text: sasif brings three key benefits clinical R programming: One condition, multiple assignments — repeated logic, QC risk conditions diverging Familiar SAS syntax — / ELSE / ELSE control flow clinical programmers already know data.table performance — fully vectorized, row loops, scales millions rows information, see package documentation.

what: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: SAS -Style Data Step Logic R — Improving readability consistency SDTM & ADaM derivations. sasif lets clinical programmers write one condition governs multiple variable assignments single block — just like SAS ... . repeated conditions. logic drift. maintenance burden.

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: traditional R, every variable assignment needs repeated condition: one condition ever updated, must find change every single line. Miss one derivation silently diverges. real QC risk regulated clinical trial data.
code: # ❌ Traditional R (case_when) — condition repeated for EVERY variable adsl <- adsl %>% mutate(   SAFFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   SAFFLN  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ 1),   TRT01A  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ ACTARMCD),   TRT01AN = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ 1),   TRTSDT  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d")),   TRTEDT  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ as.Date(RFENDTC, "%Y-%m-%d")),   ITTFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   FASFL   = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   RANDFL  = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y"),   PPFL    = case_when(ACTARMCD == "TRTA" ~ "Y")   # condition repeated 10 times — high QC risk if any diverge )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: One condition. assignments grouped together. Just like SAS: Clean. Readable. Audit-friendly. Identical intent SAS ... .
code: # ✅ sasif — condition written ONCE, governs all assignments library(sasif)  ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTA",     SAFFL   = "Y",     SAFFLN  = 1,     TRT01A  = ACTARMCD,     TRT01AN = 1,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     TRTEDT  = as.Date(RFENDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     ITTFL   = "Y",     FASFL   = "Y",     RANDFL  = "Y",     PPFL    = "Y"   ) )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Note: sasif requires data.table input. Convert setDT(df) calling data_step().
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("sasif")  # Install development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("chandrt23-lang/sasif")

what: Example 1 — ADSL: Multiple Population Flags in One Block
title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: common use case — one treatment condition drives 10 variable assignments simultaneously:
code: ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTA",     SAFFL   = "Y",     SAFFLN  = 1,     TRT01A  = ACTARMCD,     TRT01AN = 1,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     TRTEDT  = as.Date(RFENDTC, "%Y-%m-%d"),     ITTFL   = "Y",     FASFL   = "Y",     RANDFL  = "Y",     PPFL    = "Y"   ) )

what: Example 2 — ADSL: Age Category with IF / ELSE IF / ELSE
title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Mutually exclusive chain — first matching condition wins, others skipped. AGECAT (character) AGECATN (numeric) derived together: Compare data.table nested fifelse() — forces repeat condition separately variable:
code: ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   if_do(AGE <= 45,     AGECAT  = "YOUNG",     AGECATN = 1   ),   else_if_do(AGE <= 70,     AGECAT  = "MIDDLE",     AGECATN = 2   ),   else_do(     AGECAT  = "OLD",     AGECATN = 3   ) ) # ❌ data.table nested fifelse — condition repeated per variable adlb[, `:=`(   AGECAT  = fifelse(AGE <= 45, "YOUNG", fifelse(AGE <= 70, "MIDDLE", "OLD")),   AGECATN = fifelse(AGE <= 45, 1L,      fifelse(AGE <= 70, 2L,       3L)) )]

what: Example 3 — ADLB: Lab Categorisation (Character + Numeric Together)
title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Derive category label numeric code one condition block:
code: out <- data_step(adlb,   if_do(LBTESTCD == "ALB" & AVAL < ANRLO,     ALBCAT  = "LOW",     ALBCATN = 1   ),   else_if_do(LBTESTCD == "ALB" & AVAL > ANRHI,     ALBCAT  = "HIGH",     ALBCATN = 2   ),   else_do(     ALBCAT  = "NORMAL",     ALBCATN = 3   ) )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Flag adverse events started treatment start date:
code: ADAE <- data_step(adae,   if_do(ASTDT >= TRTSDT & ASTDT <= TRTEDT,     TRTEMFL = "Y",     TRTEMA  = AEDECOD   ) )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Assign treatment label, numeric code, start date together per arm:
code: ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTA",     TRT01A  = "Treatment A",     TRT01AN = 1,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d")   ),   else_if_do(ACTARMCD == "TRTB",     TRT01A  = "Treatment B",     TRT01AN = 2,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d")   ),   else_do(     TRT01A  = "Placebo",     TRT01AN = 99,     TRTSDT  = as.Date(RFSTDTC, "%Y-%m-%d")   ) )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Use if_independent() conditions mutually exclusive — evaluated , multiple flags can apply row: Important: mix if_do() chains if_independent() variable — if_independent() runs chain overwrite . Use one approach consistently per variable.
code: out <- data_step(adlb,   if_independent(AVAL < ANRLO,  LOWNFL = "Y"),   if_independent(AVAL > ANRHI,  HINFL  = "Y"),   if_independent(LBTESTCD == "ALB", ALBFL = "Y") )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Remove screen failures unscheduled visits explicitly:
code: # Remove screen failure subjects ADSL <- data_step(adsl,   delete_if(ACTARMCD == "SCRNFAIL") )  # Remove records with missing test codes and unscheduled visits ADLB <- data_step(adlb,   delete_if(is.na(LBTESTCD)),   delete_if(VISIT == "UNSCHEDULED") )

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: Deriving multiple population flags (SAFFL, ITTFL, FASFL, RANDFL) one condition Multi-variable ADaM derivations condition governs several assignments Migrating SAS ... blocks directly R QC-sensitive code auditability readability matter

title: SAS IF Style Data Step Logic for Data Tables
text: sasif focused conditional derivation logic. Use standard R packages : RETAIN / stateful accumulation → use data.table base R LAG / previous-row values → use shift() data.table ARRAY processing → use lapply data.table column operations -group stateful logic → use data.table = syntax

what: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
title: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
text: Provides SAS-style /ELSE chains, independent rules, DELETE logic fast, vectorized transformations data.table objects. enables clinical programmers express SDTM ADaM-style derivations familiar SAS-like syntax leveraging data.table performance.

title: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
code: data_step(dt, ..., copy = TRUE)

title: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
text: dt data.table. ... One rule objects created if_do(), else_if_do(), else_do(), if_independent(), delete_if(). copy Logical. TRUE (default), copy dt modified returned.

title: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
text: data.table applied transformations.

title: SAS IF-style data step logic for data.table — data_step
code: library(data.table) #> Warning: package 'data.table' was built under R version 4.5.2  dt <- data.table(   AGE = c(40, 60, 80),   SEX = c("M", "F", "M") )  out <- data_step(   dt,   if_do(AGE <= 45, GROUP = 1),   else_if_do(AGE <= 70, GROUP = 2),   else_do(GROUP = 3),   if_independent(SEX == "M", MALE = 1) )  out #>      AGE    SEX GROUP  MALE #>    <num> <char> <num> <num> #> 1:    40      M     1     1 #> 2:    60      F     2    NA #> 3:    80      M     3     1
